senses.definition: used to provide a single-word summary or description of something
used to signal the end of a discussion or presentation by summarizing the main points

examples.x: In conclusion, the research shows that exercise has numerous health benefits.
We have examined the impact of climate change on various ecosystems; in conclusion, urgent global action is needed to address these environmental challenges.
After analyzing the data and considering different perspectives, we can, in conclusion, assert that the economic benefits of the proposed policy outweigh its potential drawbacks.
Throughout this essay, we have explored the historical context of the conflict; in conclusion, understanding these historical factors is crucial for finding a sustainable resolution.

senses.definition: used to get to the most important parts of something

examples.x: In essence, the project's success depends on effective teamwork and communication.
In essence, the proposal aims to streamline our operations and increase efficiency.
In essence, the message of the speech was about the importance of unity and cooperation.
In essence, the book explores the human condition and the search for meaning in life.
In essence, the debate centers around the balance between individual rights and societal responsibilities.

senses.definition: used to provide an alternative or clearer way of expressing the same idea

examples.x: The weather is quite pleasant today; in other words, it's a beautiful day.
She's a workaholic— in other words, she's always working.
He's frugal with his money; in other words, he's careful about how he spends it.
The team needs to work more efficiently; in other words, they need to get the job done faster.
The assignment requires creativity; in other words, you need to think outside the box.

senses.definition: in a way that efficiently captures essential details without unnecessary elaboration

examples.x: In short, the project aims to improve efficiency through automation.
The movie, in short, is a captivating thriller with unexpected twists.
In short, the new policy promotes sustainability in the workplace.
The instructions, in short, outline the steps for assembling the furniture.
In short, the novel explores themes of love, loss, and redemption.

senses.definition: used to provide a brief and straightforward explanation of the main points or ideas

word_list.pronunciation: tə pˌʊt ɪt sˈɪmpli ɔːɹ mˈaɪldli ɔːɹ bɹˈiːfli

senses.definition: used to introduce a simplified version of a statement

examples.x: To put it simply, the project was a complete success.
To put it mildly, the weather yesterday was terrible—it was pouring rain all day.
To put it briefly, the company's financial situation is precarious, and we need to take action immediately.
To put it simply, she's the most qualified candidate for the job.
To put it mildly, the traffic during rush hour is horrendous.
To put it briefly, the meeting was canceled due to unforeseen circumstances.

senses.definition: used to provide a general summary of a situation

examples.x: All in all, the vacation was enjoyable despite the rainy days.
All in all, the event was a success, despite a few minor setbacks.
All in all, it's been a challenging year, but we've managed to overcome many obstacles.
All in all, the project exceeded expectations and delivered impressive results.
All in all, it was a productive meeting, and we made significant progress on the agenda items.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something is mostly the case or generally true

examples.x: By and large, people in this town are friendly and welcoming.
By and large, the team is satisfied with the progress we've made on the project.
By and large, the company's policies are fair and equitable for all employees.
By and large, the feedback from customers has been positive since we implemented the new system.
By and large, the event was well-organized and attended by a diverse group of participants.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something is generally true or applies in the majority of cases

examples.x: For the most part, the employees are satisfied with their work environment.
For the most part, the weather in this region is sunny and pleasant.
For the most part, the company's products are of high quality and reliable.
For the most part, the event was well-attended and enjoyed by all who participated.
For the most part, people in this neighborhood are friendly and welcoming.

senses.definition: used to provide a general assessment of a situation

examples.x: On the whole, the conference was informative and well-organized.
On the whole, the project was successful, although there were a few challenges along the way.
On the whole, the company's performance has improved significantly over the past year.
On the whole, the neighborhood is a peaceful and pleasant place to live.
On the whole, the feedback from customers has been positive, with only a few minor complaints.
